name: Puig de Randa: Three Sanctuaries Trail
name_original: Santuarios de Cura y Nostra Senyora de Gràcia — circular por el Puig de Randa
description: Hike the moderate 5.7-mile circular route to the Sanctuaries of Cura and Nostra Senyora de Gràcia, enjoying panoramic views from Puig de Ran
region_intro: Hiking around Algaida offers diverse landscapes characterized by rolling countryside, lowlands, and a series of small hills, with Puig de Randa being the most prominent at 543 meters. The region features almond fields, rustic fincas, pine forests, and scrubland. Traditional 18th and 19th-century windmills are also iconic features of the area. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Algaida The most popular hiking route is Puig de Randa: Three Sanctuaries Trail, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route encompasses three historic sanctuaries and offers continuous panoramic views across the island. Another top favourite among local hikers is Historisches Waschhaus – Sanctuary of Cura loop from Puig de Randa, a moderate 3.2 miles (5.1 km) path. This trail leads to the historic Santuari de Cura, providing scenic views from the summit of Puig de Randa. Local hikers also love the Historisches Waschhaus – Randa Cross loop from Puig de Randa, a 2.1 miles (3.4 km) trail leading through the Puig de Randa area, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Hiking around Algaida is defined by rolling countryside, pine forests, and small hills, including the prominent Puig de Randa. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy excursions to moderate ascents. The routes in Algaida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Algaida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Algaida?
A: Algaida offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with over 50 trails available. These range from easy strolls through the countryside to more moderate ascents up the prominent Puig de Randa.

Q: What kind of landscape can I expect to see while hiking in Algaida?
A: Hiking in Algaida reveals a picturesque rural landscape characterized by rolling countryside, lowlands, and a series of small hills, with Puig de Randa being the highest point. You'll encounter almond fields, rustic fincas, pine forests, and scrubland. The area is also dotted with traditional 18th and 19th-century windmills, adding to its unique charm.

Q: Are there easy hiking options in Algaida?
A: Yes, Algaida offers 24 easy hiking routes, perfect for a relaxed outing. These often feature practically flat landscapes with gentle slopes, passing through pine forests and scrubland. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/991731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historisches Waschhaus – Randa Cross loop from Puig de Randa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.1 miles (3.4 km) long.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Algaida?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Algaida are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000296691/puig-de-randa-three-sanctuaries-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de Randa: Three Sanctuaries Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.7 miles (9.2 km) long and encompasses three historic sanctuaries.

Q: What notable landmarks or attractions can I visit while hiking near Algaida?
A: The most prominent landmark is &lt;strong&gt;Puig de Randa&lt;/strong&gt;, which hosts three significant sanctuaries: the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/79390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuari de Cura&lt;/a&gt; at the summit, the beautiful 15th-century Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Gracia, and the 14th-century Santuario de Sant Honorat. You can also spot traditional windmills like Molí d'en Pau throughout the countryside.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Algaida?
A: The trails in Algaida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers often praise the continuous panoramic views, especially from Puig de Randa, and the diverse scenery that includes almond fields, pine forests, and historic sites.

Q: Is Algaida suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Algaida offers several family-friendly hiking options. The varied terrain includes many easy routes with gentle slopes, making them suitable for families. The opportunity to explore historic windmills and enjoy the rural scenery provides an engaging experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Algaida?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on every trail can vary, the rural and natural character of Algaida generally makes it a welcoming area for dog walkers. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially near livestock or protected areas, and to carry water for them.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Algaida?
A: The best time to hike in Algaida is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winter offers cooler temperatures but can be damp.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails in Algaida?
A: Yes, the town of Algaida itself offers amenities including cafes and restaurants. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/79390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuari de Cura&lt;/a&gt; at the summit of Puig de Randa has a restaurant and accommodation, providing a convenient stop for refreshments during your hike.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach hiking areas in Algaida?
A: Algaida is a rural town, and while there are public transport connections to the town itself, reaching specific trailheads, especially those further into the countryside or up Puig de Randa, might require a car. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to Algaida and then plan for potential short taxi rides or walks to the starting points of trails.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Algaida?
A: Parking is generally available in the town of Algaida. For trails leading up Puig de Randa, there are parking areas near the base or even closer to the sanctuaries, allowing for shorter walks to the summit. Always look for designated parking spots to ensure you're not obstructing local access or private property.
tips: This striking cross is located shortly before the ascent to the Klosterberg. A little further up you can admire the Creu des Santuari de Gràcia. this is a listed cross in front of the sanctuary Santuari de Nostra Senyora de Gràcia.

Randa Mountain is home not only to the famous Cura Monastery at its summit, but also to a total of three monasteries scattered along the slope. The lowest is the Santuari de Gracia. Once you've mastered the challenging but scenic climb to this point, you'll definitely be grateful for a photo break. And it's definitely worth it.

On the mountain Randa there is not only the well-known monastery Cura on the summit, but altogether three monasteries, distributed on the slope. The middle one is the Ermita de Sant Honorat, which offers a great view and a lovely courtyard, as well as a picnic area with seating and table.
On the hike to the monastery mountain https://www.komoot.de/tour/163162455/embed you will pass this great viewpoint. From here you have a great view of the southeast of Mallorca.
Interlocking metal crosses by the sculptor Jaume Falconer.

Well-known excursion and hiking destination, accordingly heavily frequented. In addition to the well-frequented gastronomy, there is also a rest area with tables and benches for a less expensive rest. It is much quieter in the monastery chapel than in the back part of the monastery with the gastronomy and the rest area.

The formation of Mount Randa:
An old legend tells that a long time ago a giant crossed the Mediterranean Sea from Algiers. He carried a basket full of stones and earth on his head, and he had tied a ship under his feet so that he would not sink. During a storm off Cabrera, both ships drifted apart and, in order not to lose his balance, he braced himself with his stick on the shore of Cala Pi. But it didn't help, he fell anyway and the contents of his basket spilled out onto the plains of Mallorca. This is where Randa Mountain came from. And from a drop of sweat that hit the ground next to the mountain, the Na Tomasa spring was born.
But that's just a legend, the real story is much scarier:
Mount Randa is hollow inside and supported by four golden pillars. Should the pillars break, not only the mountain but all of Mallorca will collapse. And not only that, "The waves of the sea will crash over Mallorca, and that will be the end of the world," says the phophecy. The Mallorcans say that three of the pillars are already broken and the fourth is damaged.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Sanctuaries of Cura and Nostra Senyora de Gràcia circular hike?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the village of Randa, which serves as a common starting point for this circular trail. From there, the route ascends towards the sanctuaries.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered suitable for most fitness levels, including families. It features a gentle ascent and varied paths, making it an enjoyable activity for many. However, the climb to Sanctuary of Our Lady of Grace can be a bit challenging, so consider your children's stamina.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is particularly beautiful. Summer can be very hot, so if hiking then, start early in the morning.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sanctuaries of Cura and Nostra Senyora de Gràcia trail?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations can vary, dogs are generally permitted on hiking trails in Mallorca, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always a good idea to bring plenty of water for your dog, especially during warmer months, as water sources on the trail might be limited.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this circular trail. Access to the natural areas and the sanctuaries themselves is generally free, though donations may be welcome at some religious sites.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this hike is varied. You'll encounter dirt tracks, sections winding through holm oak and pine forests, and some paved roads. While mostly accessible, some parts require sure-footedness, especially on the ascent to the sanctuaries.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is known as the 'Mountain of the three Sanctuaries.' You'll pass by the 15th-century Sanctuary of Our Lady of Grace, the Ermita de Sant Honorat with its sweeping views, and finally the famous Sanctuary of Cura, Puig de Randa at the summit, which has a chapel, museum, and restaurant.

faqs.question: Are there any good viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The trail offers breathtaking panoramic views, especially from the summit of Puig de Randa. From the View from Puig de Randa, you can see the vast southern plain of Mallorca, the Serra de Tramuntana mountain range, and on clear days, even the islands of Cabrera and Ibiza.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route also passes through sections of the [CIMA IB02] Santuari de Cura * Montuiri and the [CIMA IB02] Santuari de Cura * Randa trails.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restaurants or cafes at the sanctuaries?
faqs.answer: Yes, at the Sanctuary of Cura, Puig de Randa, located at the summit, you will find a restaurant. The Ermita de Sant Honorat also often features a lovely courtyard and picnic area, perfect for a break.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. A camera is also recommended for the stunning views. Layers of clothing are useful as temperatures can change with elevation.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Grace
Sanctuary of Cura, Puig de Randa
